{"projectId":"13332484261109626353","sessionId":"16752086432010441532","outputComponents":[{"design":{"screens":[{"screenshot":{"name":"projects/13332484261109626353/files/85b2a4961b084b0980e5eeda7c7bf7e8","downloadUrl":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/aida/ADBb0ui_RNIG9Puivc-RS8CHwQdACpcqqpycXx8yYFJBdbxC5SEFFOwNBr1vZD2Xo483ntH0Rx2pQ_hLLs3RksXFWDrVTYrbBpRviwSi37tM0V0SS4NfwWCSccc1nQylWLBHasMG3C3-lTqgDINFuSlQP8ecOIPBD2Vd2ApPmWRlCjremjlUap-yxNK04JY_i4W7bwgnwjm-ZYwK_6Fhzrq9zUWE4Fxqa-i0XEYeUdiTdb40p_90S2EjvNPyIgII"},"htmlCode":{"name":"projects/13332484261109626353/files/f9545be040144708a4b148148d007262","downloadUrl":"https://contribution.usercontent.google.com/download?c=CgthaWRhX2NvZGVmeBJ8Eh1hcHBfY29tcGFuaW9uX2dlbmVyYXRlZF9maWxlcxpbCiVodG1sXzMzY2FlMTY3NjA2MDQ5NWU4MTZiODI2MmZkOWMwMGY3EgsSBxCg9KiWmAMYAZIBJAoKcHJvamVjdF9pZBIWQhQxMzMzMjQ4NDI2MTEwOTYyNjM1Mw&filename=&opi=96797242","mimeType":"text/html"},"id":"8ac3b8277334437ca3cbd3ad97b26a29","generatedBy":"figaro_agent","width":"2560","height":"2644","title":"Optimización tarifa/potencia — EnergyOS","prompt":"Crea una pantalla de administración para EnergyOS titulada "Optimización de Tarifa y Potencia". Esta es una herramienta secundaria para gestoras de apartamentos turísticos.

La pantalla debe incluir:
1. SideNavBar con las secciones: Dashboard, Apartamentos, Operaciones, Informes, Tarifa/Potencia (activa), Leads, Configuración.
2. TopNavBar con el nombre de la gestora "Costa Norte Stays" y búsqueda de apartamentos.
3. Encabezado de página: Título "Optimización de Tarifa y Potencia" y un subtítulo que indique que es un módulo complementario enfocado en la eficiencia estructural.
4. Resumen de cartera: Una card lateral con métricas clave: Apartamentos analizados (5), Acciones pendientes (3), Ahorro potencial total (584€ / año).
5. Sección principal: "Análisis de Potencia Recomendada".
   - Detalle para VGO-014 · Apartamento Areal.
   - Gráfico simple comparando Potencia Actual (4.6 kW) vs Recomendada (3.3 kW).
   - Card de recomendación: "Bajar potencia contratada en periodo valle (P2)".
   - Ahorro estimado: 12€/mes (145€ anuales).
   - Botón de acción: "Aplicar cambio".
6. Tabla comparativa de cartera:
   - Columnas: Apartamento, Tarifa Actual, Potencia (P1/P2), Recomendación, Ahorro Est., Estado.
   - Filas con los apartamentos de muestra:
     - VGO-014: Endesa One 2.0TD | 4.6 / 4.6 kW | Bajar P2 a 3.3 kW | 145€/año | Pendiente.
     - SCQ-022: Iberdrola Plan Estable | 3.3 / 3.3 kW | Optimizado | - | Completado.
     - LCG-008: Naturgy Por Uso | 5.7 / 5.7 kW | Bajar P1/P2 a 4.6 kW | 210€/año | Pendiente.
     - SNX-031: TotalEnergies | 4.6 / 4.6 kW | Cambio a Tarifa Indexada | 112€/año | En trámite.
     - BAI-006: Endesa Tempo 24h | 3.3 / 3.3 kW | Bajar P2 a 2.3 kW | 117€/año | Pendiente.
7. Nota informativa al pie: Explicar que los ahorros son estimaciones basadas en el consumo histórico de los últimos 12 meses.

Restricciones visuales: Estilo Linear/Stripe, modo claro, tipografía Inter, bordes suaves (#E5E7EB), colores profesionales (Azul #2563EB para acciones, Verde #10B981 para optimizados). NO mencionar baterías, autoconsumo ni trading. ## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for high-utility B2B operations in the hospitality sector. It follows a **Minimalist / Modern Corporate** aesthetic inspired by industry leaders like Linear and Stripe, prioritizing information density without sacrificing clarity. 

The visual language communicates reliability, precision, and speed. It uses ample whitespace to reduce the cognitive load of managing multiple properties, while subtle borders and a restricted color palette ensure that the user's focus remains on operational data and actionable insights.

## Colors
The palette is strictly functional, utilizing a high-contrast foundation for maximum legibility. 
- **Core:** The primary interface relies on `#F8FAFC` for background depth and pure white for interactive containers (Cards).
- **Typography:** Primary text uses a deep navy-charcoal (`#0F172A`) to ensure WCAG AAA compliance, while secondary metadata uses a muted slate (`#64748B`).
- **Semantics:** Color is reserved for state communication. Teal signals successful check-ins or clean units; Amber indicates pending guest actions or pre-check-in windows; Red is strictly for critical alerts (e.g., overstays or maintenance emergencies); Blue handles secondary systemic actions and neutral progress.

## Typography
This design system uses **Inter** exclusively to leverage its exceptional legibility in data-heavy environments. 
- **Scale:** A tight scale is used to maintain a professional, "dashboard" feel. 
- **Hierarchy:** We use weight (Medium 500 and Semi-Bold 600) rather than drastic size increases to differentiate information. 
- **Numerical Data:** Tabular figures and dates should utilize Inter’s "tnum" (tabular figures) OpenType feature to ensure columns of numbers align perfectly in management tables.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed-Fluid Hybrid** model. 
- **Sidebar:** A solid navigation bar is fixed to the left at 260px. It uses a slightly darker or textured background (or the primary text color with low-opacity items) to anchor the application.
- **Main Canvas:** Content sits within a fluid container with a max-width of 1440px to prevent excessive line lengths on ultra-wide monitors.
- **Grid:** We utilize an 8px base grid. Tables and lists use "md" (16px) internal padding for a dense but breathable feel. Margins between cards are consistently 24px ("lg").

## Elevation & Depth
In this design system, depth is conveyed through **Low-contrast outlines** rather than heavy shadows.
- **Level 0 (Background):** `#F8FAFC`
- **Level 1 (Cards/Surface):** White background with a 1px solid border of `#E5E7EB`. No shadow is used for static cards.
- **Level 2 (Interactive/Dropdowns):** White background with a subtle, diffused ambient shadow (`0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)`) and a border.
- **Overlays:** Modals use a background blur (8px) on the backdrop to maintain the "clean" aesthetic while isolating the task.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Soft** and restrained. 
- **Standard UI elements** (Buttons, Inputs, Cards) use a 4px (0.25rem) radius to maintain a professional, geometric look. 
- **Badges and Tags** use a slightly more rounded 6px radius or are fully pill-shaped to distinguish them from interactive buttons.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke width icons with slightly rounded caps to match the typography.

## Components
- **Navigation:** A solid lateral sidebar with clear active states using a small left-accent bar in Primary Blue or a subtle background tint.
- **Tables:** No vertical borders. Horizontal dividers only in `#E5E7EB`. Header row uses `label-sm` with `text-secondary`.
- **Badges:** Small, high-radius containers for status. Use "Subtle" styling (e.g., Success: Light green background with dark green text) for general status, and "Solid" styling for critical alerts.
- **Timeline:** A vertical thread connecting circles. Use the semantic color palette for the circles to indicate the status of each step in the guest journey (Booking -> Pre-check-in -> Active -> Checkout).
- **Buttons:** 
  - *Primary:* Solid `#0F172A` with white text.
  - *Secondary:* Outline with `#E5E7EB` border and `#0F172A` text.
  - *Ghost:* No border, `#64748B` text, appearing on hover.
- **Inputs:** 1px border `#E5E7EB`. ## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for high-utility B2B operations in the hospitality sector. It follows a **Minimalist / Modern Corporate** aesthetic inspired by industry leaders like Linear and Stripe, prioritizing information density without sacrificing clarity. 

The visual language communicates reliability, precision, and speed. It uses ample whitespace to reduce the cognitive load of managing multiple properties, while subtle borders and a restricted color palette ensure that the user's focus remains on operational data and actionable insights.

## Colors
The palette is strictly functional, utilizing a high-contrast foundation for maximum legibility. 
- **Core:** The primary interface relies on `#F8FAFC` for background depth and pure white for interactive containers (Cards).
- **Typography:** Primary text uses a deep navy-charcoal (`#0F172A`) to ensure WCAG AAA compliance, while secondary metadata uses a muted slate (`#64748B`).
- **Semantics:** Color is reserved for state communication. Teal signals successful check-ins or clean units; Amber indicates pending guest actions or pre-check-in windows; Red is strictly for critical alerts (e.g., overstays or maintenance emergencies); Blue handles secondary systemic actions and neutral progress.

## Typography
This design system uses **Inter** exclusively to leverage its exceptional legibility in data-heavy environments. 
- **Scale:** A tight scale is used to maintain a professional, "dashboard" feel. 
- **Hierarchy:** We use weight (Medium 500 and Semi-Bold 600) rather than drastic size increases to differentiate information. 
- **Numerical Data:** Tabular figures and dates should utilize Inter’s "tnum" (tabular figures) OpenType feature to ensure columns of numbers align perfectly in management tables.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed-Fluid Hybrid** model. 
- **Sidebar:** A solid navigation bar is fixed to the left at 260px. It uses a slightly darker or textured background (or the primary text color with low-opacity items) to anchor the application.
- **Main Canvas:** Content sits within a fluid container with a max-width of 1440px to prevent excessive line lengths on ultra-wide monitors.
- **Grid:** We utilize an 8px base grid. Tables and lists use "md" (16px) internal padding for a dense but breathable feel. Margins between cards are consistently 24px ("lg").

## Elevation & Depth
In this design system, depth is conveyed through **Low-contrast outlines** rather than heavy shadows.
- **Level 0 (Background):** `#F8FAFC`
- **Level 1 (Cards/Surface):** White background with a 1px solid border of `#E5E7EB`. No shadow is used for static cards.
- **Level 2 (Interactive/Dropdowns):** White background with a subtle, diffused ambient shadow (`0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)`) and a border.
- **Overlays:** Modals use a background blur (8px) on the backdrop to maintain the "clean" aesthetic while isolating the task.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Soft** and restrained. 
- **Standard UI elements** (Buttons, Inputs, Cards) use a 4px (0.25rem) radius to maintain a professional, geometric look. 
- **Badges and Tags** use a slightly more rounded 6px radius or are fully pill-shaped to distinguish them from interactive buttons.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke width icons with slightly rounded caps to match the typography.

## Components
- **Navigation:** A solid lateral sidebar with clear active states using a small left-accent bar in Primary Blue or a subtle background tint.
- **Tables:** No vertical borders. Horizontal dividers only in `#E5E7EB`. Header row uses `label-sm` with `text-secondary`.
- **Badges:** Small, high-radius containers for status. Use "Subtle" styling (e.g., Success: Light green background with dark green text) for general status, and "Solid" styling for critical alerts.
- **Timeline:** A vertical thread connecting circles. Use the semantic color palette for the circles to indicate the status of each step in the guest journey (Booking -> Pre-check-in -> Active -> Checkout).
- **Buttons:** 
  - *Primary:* Solid `#0F172A` with white text.
  - *Secondary:* Outline with `#E5E7EB` border and `#0F172A` text.
  - *Ghost:* No border, `#64748B` text, appearing on hover.
- **Inputs:** 1px border `#E5E7EB`. ### Aspectos clave del diseño:
- **Análisis de Potencia:** He incluido un módulo destacado para el apartamento **VGO-014**, comparando visualmente la potencia contratada actual frente a la recomendada para reducir costes fijos.
- **Tabla Comparativa:** Una vista detallada de toda la cartera (**Costa Norte Stays**) que identifica oportunidades de ahorro específicas por cada propiedad, ya sea ajustando potencias o sugiriendo cambios a tarifas indexadas.
- **Métricas de Cartera:** Un panel lateral resume el impacto económico total (584€/año), aportando valor al gestor sin desviar la atención de la operativa diaria.

designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for high-utility B2B operations in the hospitality sector. It follows a **Minimalist / Modern Corporate** aesthetic inspired by industry leaders like Linear and Stripe, prioritizing information density without sacrificing clarity. 

The visual language communicates reliability, precision, and speed. It uses ample whitespace to reduce the cognitive load of managing multiple properties, while subtle borders and a restricted color palette ensure that the user's focus remains on operational data and actionable insights.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed-Fluid Hybrid** model. 
- **Sidebar:** A solid navigation bar is fixed to the left at 260px. It uses a slightly darker or textured background (or the primary text color with low-opacity items) to anchor the application.
- **Main Canvas:** Content sits within a fluid container with a max-width of 1440px to prevent excessive line lengths on ultra-wide monitors.
- **Grid:** We utilize an 8px base grid. Tables and lists use "md" (16px) internal padding for a dense but breathable feel. Margins between cards are consistently 24px ("lg").

## Elevation & Depth
In this design system, depth is conveyed through **Low-contrast outlines** rather than heavy shadows.
- **Level 0 (Background):** `#F8FAFC`
- **Level 1 (Cards/Surface):** White background with a 1px solid border of `#E5E7EB`. No shadow is used for static cards.
- **Level 2 (Interactive/Dropdowns):** White background with a subtle, diffused ambient shadow (`0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)`) and a border.
- **Overlays:** Modals use a background blur (8px) on the backdrop to maintain the "clean" aesthetic while isolating the task.

## Components
- **Navigation:** A solid lateral sidebar with clear active states using a small left-accent bar in Primary Blue or a subtle background tint.
- **Tables:** No vertical borders. Horizontal dividers only in `#E5E7EB`. Header row uses `label-sm` with `text-secondary`.
- **Badges:** Small, high-radius containers for status. Use "Subtle" styling (e.g., Success: Light green background with dark green text) for general status, and "Solid" styling for critical alerts.
- **Timeline:** A vertical thread connecting circles. Use the semantic color palette for the circles to indicate the status of each step in the guest journey (Booking -> Pre-check-in -> Active -> Checkout).
- **Buttons:** 
  - *Primary:* Solid `#0F172A` with white text.
  - *Secondary:* Outline with `#E5E7EB` border and `#0F172A` text.
  - *Ghost:* No border, `#64748B` text, appearing on hover.
- **Inputs:** 1px border `#E5E7EB`. On focus, use a 1px solid `#2563EB` border with a subtle 2px blue glow (ring).

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for high-utility B2B operations in the hospitality sector. It follows a **Minimalist / Modern Corporate** aesthetic inspired by industry leaders like Linear and Stripe, prioritizing information density without sacrificing clarity. 

The visual language communicates reliability, precision, and speed. It uses ample whitespace to reduce the cognitive load of managing multiple properties, while subtle borders and a restricted color palette ensure that the user's focus remains on operational data and actionable insights.

## Colors
The palette is strictly functional, utilizing a high-contrast foundation for maximum legibility. 
- **Core:** The primary interface relies on `#F8FAFC` for background depth and pure white for interactive containers (Cards).
- **Typography:** Primary text uses a deep navy-charcoal (`#0F172A`) to ensure WCAG AAA compliance, while secondary metadata uses a muted slate (`#64748B`).
- **Semantics:** Color is reserved for state communication. Teal signals successful check-ins or clean units; Amber indicates pending guest actions or pre-check-in windows; Red is strictly for critical alerts (e.g., overstays or maintenance emergencies); Blue handles secondary systemic actions and neutral progress.

## Typography
This design system uses **Inter** exclusively to leverage its exceptional legibility in data-heavy environments. 
- **Scale:** A tight scale is used to maintain a professional, "dashboard" feel. 
- **Hierarchy:** We use weight (Medium 500 and Semi-Bold 600) rather than drastic size increases to differentiate information. 
- **Numerical Data:** Tabular figures and dates should utilize Inter’s "tnum" (tabular figures) OpenType feature to ensure columns of numbers align perfectly in management tables.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed-Fluid Hybrid** model. 
- **Sidebar:** A solid navigation bar is fixed to the left at 260px. It uses a slightly darker or textured background (or the primary text color with low-opacity items) to anchor the application.
- **Main Canvas:** Content sits within a fluid container with a max-width of 1440px to prevent excessive line lengths on ultra-wide monitors.
- **Grid:** We utilize an 8px base grid. Tables and lists use "md" (16px) internal padding for a dense but breathable feel. Margins between cards are consistently 24px ("lg").

## Elevation & Depth
In this design system, depth is conveyed through **Low-contrast outlines** rather than heavy shadows.
- **Level 0 (Background):** `#F8FAFC`
- **Level 1 (Cards/Surface):** White background with a 1px solid border of `#E5E7EB`. No shadow is used for static cards.
- **Level 2 (Interactive/Dropdowns):** White background with a subtle, diffused ambient shadow (`0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)`) and a border.
- **Overlays:** Modals use a background blur (8px) on the backdrop to maintain the "clean" aesthetic while isolating the task.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Soft** and restrained. 
- **Standard UI elements** (Buttons, Inputs, Cards) use a 4px (0.25rem) radius to maintain a professional, geometric look. 
- **Badges and Tags** use a slightly more rounded 6px radius or are fully pill-shaped to distinguish them from interactive buttons.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke width icons with slightly rounded caps to match the typography.

## Components
- **Navigation:** A solid lateral sidebar with clear active states using a small left-accent bar in Primary Blue or a subtle background tint.
- **Tables:** No vertical borders. Horizontal dividers only in `#E5E7EB`. Header row uses `label-sm` with `text-secondary`.
- **Badges:** Small, high-radius containers for status. Use "Subtle" styling (e.g., Success: Light green background with dark green text) for general status, and "Solid" styling for critical alerts.
- **Timeline:** A vertical thread connecting circles. Use the semantic color palette for the circles to indicate the status of each step in the guest journey (Booking -> Pre-check-in -> Active -> Checkout).
- **Buttons:** 
  - *Primary:* Solid `#0F172A` with white text.
  - *Secondary:* Outline with `#E5E7EB` border and `#0F172A` text.
  - *Ghost:* No border, `#64748B` text, appearing on hover.
- **Inputs:** 1px border `#E5E7EB`. On focus, use a 1px solid `#2563EB` border with a subtle 2px blue glow (ring).

screens.name: projects/13332484261109626353/screens/8ac3b8277334437ca3cbd3ad97b26a29
screens.prompt:
Crea una pantalla de administración para EnergyOS titulada "Optimización de Tarifa y Potencia". Esta es una herramienta secundaria para gestoras de apartamentos turísticos.

La pantalla debe incluir:
1. SideNavBar con las secciones: Dashboard, Apartamentos, Operaciones, Informes, Tarifa/Potencia (activa), Leads, Configuración.
2. TopNavBar con el nombre de la gestora "Costa Norte Stays" y búsqueda de apartamentos.
3. Encabezado de página: Título "Optimización de Tarifa y Potencia" y un subtítulo que indique que es un módulo complementario enfocado en la eficiencia estructural.
4. Resumen de cartera: Una card lateral con métricas clave: Apartamentos analizados (5), Acciones pendientes (3), Ahorro potencial total (584€ / año).
5. Sección principal: "Análisis de Potencia Recomendada".
   - Detalle para VGO-014 · Apartamento Areal.
   - Gráfico simple comparando Potencia Actual (4.6 kW) vs Recomendada (3.3 kW).
   - Card de recomendación: "Bajar potencia contratada en periodo valle (P2)".
   - Ahorro estimado: 12€/mes (145€ anuales).
   - Botón de acción: "Aplicar cambio".
6. Tabla comparativa de cartera:
   - Columnas: Apartamento, Tarifa Actual, Potencia (P1/P2), Recomendación, Ahorro Est., Estado.
   - Filas con los apartamentos de muestra:
     - VGO-014: Endesa One 2.0TD | 4.6 / 4.6 kW | Bajar P2 a 3.3 kW | 145€/año | Pendiente.
     - SCQ-022: Iberdrola Plan Estable | 3.3 / 3.3 kW | Optimizado | - | Completado.
     - LCG-008: Naturgy Por Uso | 5.7 / 5.7 kW | Bajar P1/P2 a 4.6 kW | 210€/año | Pendiente.
     - SNX-031: TotalEnergies | 4.6 / 4.6 kW | Cambio a Tarifa Indexada | 112€/año | En trámite.
     - BAI-006: Endesa Tempo 24h | 3.3 / 3.3 kW | Bajar P2 a 2.3 kW | 117€/año | Pendiente.
7. Nota informativa al pie: Explicar que los ahorros son estimaciones basadas en el consumo histórico de los últimos 12 meses.

Restricciones visuales: Estilo Linear/Stripe, modo claro, tipografía Inter, bordes suaves (#E5E7EB), colores profesionales (Azul #2563EB para acciones, Verde #10B981 para optimizados). NO mencionar baterías, autoconsumo ni trading. Todo en castellano.

screens.title: Optimización tarifa/potencia — EnergyOS

outputComponents.text:
He diseñado la pantalla de **Optimización de Tarifa y Potencia** para el MVP de EnergyOS.

Esta versión se centra exclusivamente en la eficiencia estructural, siguiendo tus directrices para mantener un enfoque operativo prudente:

### Aspectos clave del diseño:
- **Análisis de Potencia:** He incluido un módulo destacado para el apartamento **VGO-014**, comparando visualmente la potencia contratada actual frente a la recomendada para reducir costes fijos.
- **Tabla Comparativa:** Una vista detallada de toda la cartera (**Costa Norte Stays**) que identifica oportunidades de ahorro específicas por cada propiedad, ya sea ajustando potencias o sugiriendo cambios a tarifas indexadas.
- **Métricas de Cartera:** Un panel lateral resume el impacto económico total (584€/año), aportando valor al gestor sin desviar la atención de la operativa diaria.
- **Estilo Operativo:** Mantengo el lenguaje visual profesional y limpio (estilo Linear), utilizando una paleta de colores coherente con los estados de alerta y éxito del resto del proyecto.

¿Te gustaría ajustar alguna de las recomendaciones o prefieres que pasemos a diseñar la **Configuración del Piloto** para definir las reglas de automatización?

outputComponents.suggestion: Configurar reglas de automatización de clima
Ajustar la tabla de comparativa de tarifas
